compiled_state.title: Write RED test: Pattern Reference section structural boundary
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Story
90e1-a0e4 — Enriched task descriptions: Pattern Reference section

## Goal
Write a failing (RED) structural boundary test that validates the Pattern Reference subsection in implementation-plan SKILL.md Step 3 before the subsection is added.

## Test File
tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-pattern-reference.sh

## Test Cases (≥3 required)
1. **test_pattern_reference_heading_present** — `### Pattern Reference` heading exists in Step 3 of SKILL.md
2. **test_pattern_reference_low_familiarity_rule** — the section describes including codebase examples for low/medium familiarity tasks (structural boundary: "low" or "medium" familiarity mentioned in the Pattern Reference subsection)
3. **test_pattern_reference_line_cap_rule** — the section mentions a 30-line (or ≤30-line) cap on retrieved codebase examples

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test file exists and is executable
- All 3 test cases FAIL before implementation (RED)
- All 3 test cases PASS after implementation (GREEN)
- Follows behavioral-testing-standard.md Rule 5: structural boundary only, no content-wording assertions
- Uses the same structure as tests/scripts/test-implementation-plan-task-description.sh

## .test-index
After creating this file, add entry to .test-index:
  plugins/dso/skills/implementation-plan/SKILL.md → tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-pattern-reference.sh [test_pattern_reference_heading_present]

## Files to Modify
- tests/skills/test-implementation-plan-pattern-reference.sh (CREATE)
- .test-index (UPDATE — add RED marker)
